programmes.show_description: Amy Lamé rounds out Saturday night, presenting 2 hours of Swedish DJ Olof Dreijer's set live from Homobloc.
(Some of) the best tracks to have soundtracked dance floors over the last 25 years!
Welcome the new season with this relaxing two-hour spring-themed mix.  Featuring music from Talk Talk, Air, Nina Simone and more.
Let AFRODEUTSCHE provide the perfect soundtrack for that first cup of coffee with music from Sunday Service Chior, Rozi Plain, Dennis Bovell and Mitski.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and chat, including highlights from this year's 6 Music Festival. With music from Yard Act, Courntey Barnett and Lynks.
Cerys complements the festival by looking at the city's canals with Dr Keith Hendry and the rise of climbing with James Lister from The Climbing Depot.
Guy looks back at the Manchester music scene of the late 90s and early 2000s, which revolved around the chippy-turned-music venue the Night and Day Cafe.
Iggy serves up his last sonic cocktail of 2026 including loads of show favourites. Expect key cuts by the likes of Sleaford Mods, Mediaeval Baebes, Albert Ayler, Miles and more.
Mary Anne wishes Iggy happy trails, as he takes a gap-year at 6 Music. Plus some music from this weekend's 6 Music Festival.
As the 6 Music Festival returns to Manchester, Stuart looks at the more experimental side of its music scene.
Zakia selects her favourite songs from film soundtracks to honour her special guests CJ Mirra and Duval Timothy, composers of the soundtrack for the film My Father's Shadow.
1/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share their favourite UK psychedelic music. Contains language which some may find offensive.
2/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share records which have shaped their career.
3/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors celebrate women in post-punk.
4/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share some of their favourite new artists.
Emily Pilbeam presents a mixtape of her personal selection of tracks from BBC Introducing, with highlights and conversation from the BBC Introducing night at the 6 Music Festival.
It's Amy Lamé in for Chris Hawkins with a stack of great tunes for your Monday morning.
Deb Grant sits in with wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Broadcaster and writer Zakia tells us about her new book Finding Albion, which is a deep dive into the nation's long-forgotten culture and traditions.
Chris shares some of his highlights from the 6 Music Festival 2026, with live music from Naima Bock and Courtney Barnett.
Steve has brand new music from Lambrini Girls and Liz Lawrence and repeats some of Bloc Party's set from the 6 Music Festival in Salford.
Nathan and Deb unveil this week's Breaking Artist, Philadelphia group Full Body 2, following the release of their single Turn So Slow.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
1/4. Kim Gordon shares some of her favourites songs, and some of the artists that inspired Sonic Youth.
